steps.action: Run canonical clean first-run invocation: `npx -y spikes-mcp` with initialize payload in isolated workspace
steps.expected: Wrapper installs, downloads platform binary, and returns MCP initialize response
steps.observed: npm returned E404 (`spikes-mcp` package not found on registry), so wrapper could not start

steps.action: Run local fallback (`npm pack` + `npx --package <tarball> spikes-mcp`) to isolate release-asset behavior
steps.expected: Fallback wrapper downloads binary and returns initialize response
steps.observed: Wrapper executed but binary download for `aarch64-apple-darwin` returned HTTP 404; MCP server did not start

issues.message: `spikes-mcp` package is not available from npm registry in this environment (E404).
Wrapper fallback cannot fetch required binary for current platform because release asset returns HTTP 404.

evidence.name: canonical_npx_spikes_mcp
evidence.output: npm ERR! 404 Not Found - GET https://registry.npmjs.org/spikes-mcp - Not found

evidence.name: fallback_npx_package_tarball
evidence.output: spikes-mcp: downloading binary for aarch64-apple-darwin... Binary not found (HTTP 404).

frictions: Registry package and release asset availability both prevented initialize-level wrapper success in clean conditions.
blockers: Publish `spikes-mcp` package and attach platform binary release artifacts before VAL-DISC-011 can pass end-to-end.
